user_query:
I'm preparing a tiny internal microsite to document a CLI. Please read the content and navigation from input/site_content.yaml and input/nav.json, then generate a 3-page static site with:
- output/site/index.html (Home)
- output/site/docs.html (Docs)
- output/site/contact.html (Contact)

Requirements:
1) Build clean, self-contained HTML pages (no external assets) with a shared header/nav so all three pages link to each other using relative links that work when served from a directory.
2) Start a local Python static server to preview the site. Validate that each page loads and the nav links work. Then document exactly how to run it in output/serve_instructions.md, including commands to serve the output/site directory on the default port 8000 and on a custom port 9000. Include the exact URLs a tester can click (e.g., http://localhost:8000/index.html, etc.) for both ports.
3) Create output/urls.json enumerating all localhost URLs for both ports (8000 and 9000) for each page.
4) Create output/server_log.txt summarizing what you did to preview locally: include the command(s) you ran and the list of URLs you visited to validate the pages. Use only a local server; do not rely on any external network resources.

Make sure all outputs are written under output/ with the exact paths above, and keep everything strictly static HTML/CSS with relative links. If you create any shared CSS, keep it inline or in output/site/ as a local file. The instructions should be clear enough that any teammate can reproduce the local preview quickly.
